# Data Layout and Path Conventions

## Directory Hierarchy

`data/` lives **outside** `PUB-framework-Alfonso/` as a sibling under `Data & Code/`. This keeps
the git repo code-only. All paths in config files should be specified relative to the
`Data & Code/` workspace root (e.g. `data/input_data/zahirovic2022/`). `main.py` resolves
them to absolute paths before passing to notebooks.

```
Data & Code/                                   ← workspace root
│
├── PUB-framework-Alfonso/                     ← this repo (code only; git-tracked)
│   ├── config/
│   │   ├── base defaults → notebook_parameters_default.yml
│   │   ├── run_baseline_clennett.yml
│   │   ├── run_baseline_zahirovic2022.yml
│   │   ├── run_mantle_v1.yml
│   │   └── run_mantle_v2.yml
│   ├── lib/
│   ├── outputs/                               ← run-isolated outputs (git-ignored)
│   │   └── {run_name}/
│   │       ├── config_snapshot.yml
│   │       ├── training_data.csv
│   │       ├── classifier_pub.joblib
│   │       ├── classifier_rf.joblib
│   │       ├── feature_importance.csv
│   │       └── probability_maps/
│   └── *.ipynb
│
└── data/                                      ← all data; sibling of repo (not git-tracked)
    ├── input_data/
    │   ├── zahirovic2022/                     ← all data tied to this reconstruction
    │   │   ├── plate_model/                   ← .rot, .gpml topology files, static polygons
    │   │   ├── rasters/                       ← rasters generated by 00a
    │   │   │   ├── SeafloorAge/
    │   │   │   │   └── seafloor_age_{NNN}Ma.nc
    │   │   │   ├── SpreadingRate/
    │   │   │   ├── SedimentThickness/
    │   │   │   ├── CarbonateThickness/
    │   │   │   ├── CrustalCO2/
    │   │   │   ├── CrustalThickness/
    │   │   │   └── ErosionDeposition/
    │   │   └── mantle_outputs/                ← G-ADOPT netCDF outputs
    │   │       ├── v1_pre_craton_fix/
    │   │       │   └── zahirovic2022_gadopt_{NNN}Ma.nc
    │   │       └── v2_post_craton_fix/
    │   │           └── zahirovic2022_gadopt_{NNN}Ma.nc
    │   ├── clennett2020/                      ← Alfonso 2024 original model (baseline)
    │   │   ├── plate_model/                   ← existing plate_model/ from fork
    │   │   └── grids/                         ← Zenodo 14010839 pre-extracted rasters
    │   └── [cao2024/]                         ← future; same structure
    ├── deposits/
    │   └── GlobalUnifiedDeposits.csv          ← reconstruction-agnostic deposit database
    └── reference_features/                    ← static, reconstruction-invariant shapefiles
        ├── regions.geojson
        └── craton_margins.shp                 ← future
```

## Extracted Data — Shared Cache

Extracted data lives **outside** `outputs/` because it depends only on the reconstruction and
reference feature, not on training configuration choices. It is a shared cache that any run
with matching parameters can reference.

```
data/extracted_data/
└── {reconstruction_name}/               e.g. zahirovic2022
    └── {reference_feature}/             e.g. trenches
        └── {buffer_km}km_buffer/        e.g. 600km_buffer
            ├── metadata.json            ← records generation parameters
            ├── study_area_polygons/
            │   └── study_area_{NNN}Ma.geojson
            ├── points/
            │   ├── labelled_deposits.csv     ← reconstructed deposit lat/lon at formation age
            │   ├── unlabelled_points.csv     ← sphere-uniform points in study area
            │   └── prediction_grid.csv       ← regular grid for prospectivity maps
            ├── subducting_plate_features.csv ← kinematics at trench points (depends on tessellation)
            ├── raster_features.csv           ← features from grids at all training points
            └── mantle_features_{gadopt_run_name}.csv  ← keyed by G-ADOPT run
```

## How Config Keys Map to Paths

`main.py` constructs all derived paths from config. Agents should never hardcode these paths.

| Derived path | Construction |
|---|---|
| `raster_data_dir` | `data/input_data/{reconstruction.name}/grids/` |
| `mantle_data_dir` | `data/input_data/{reconstruction.name}/mantle_outputs/{gadopt_run_name}/` |
| `extracted_data_dir` | `data/extracted_data/{reconstruction.name}/{reference_feature}/{buffer_km}km_buffer/` |
| `output_dir` | `outputs/{run_name}/` (inside repo, git-ignored) |
| `plate_model_dir` | `data/input_data/{reconstruction.name}/plate_model/` |

The notebooks read `extracted_data_dir`, `raster_data_dir`, and `output_dir` from the resolved
YAML config written by `main.py`. They should never construct these paths themselves.

## Deposit Database Schema

Source: `cu-deposits-preprocessing/` → `data/deposits/GlobalUnifiedDeposits.csv`

Key columns used by the pipeline:

| Column | Used for |
|---|---|
| `Lat`, `Lon` | Present-day coordinates |
| `Recon_Age_Ma` | Reconstruction age (null→0, plate-age corrected) |
| `Plate_ID` | GPlates plate ID (assigned by preprocessing pipeline) |
| `Ore_Tonnage_Mt` | Available for sample weighting (not yet activated) |
| `Cu_Tonnage_Mt` | Available for sample weighting (maps to `Cu (Mt)` in `lib/pu.py`) |
| `DepositGroup` | Porphyry / VMS / SedCu / IOCG |
| `Age_Mod_Flag` | 0=unchanged, 1=null→0, 2=plate-age corrected |

Alfonso's `deposits.csv` schema: `lon, lat, age (Ma), label, source`.
Map: `Lon→lon`, `Lat→lat`, `Recon_Age_Ma→age (Ma)`, `label="positive"`, `Source→source`.
Include `Ore_Tonnage_Mt` and `Cu_Tonnage_Mt` as passthrough columns for future weighting.

## File Naming Conventions

- Raster grids: `{variable}_{NNN}Ma.nc` where `NNN` is zero-padded to 3 digits (e.g. `seafloor_age_010Ma.nc`)
- G-ADOPT mantle outputs: `zahirovic2022_gadopt_{NNN}Ma.nc`
- Study area polygons: `study_area_{NNN}Ma.geojson`
- Config snapshots: `config_snapshot.yml` — always written to `outputs/{run_name}/` by `main.py`
- Mantle feature CSVs: `mantle_features_{gadopt_run_name}.csv` — explicit run name avoids overwriting

## Git Strategy

- `lib/`, notebooks, `config/`, `docs/`, `.github/` → committed
- `outputs/` → inside repo but git-ignored (add to `.gitignore`)
- `data/` → sibling of repo at `Data & Code/data/`; never inside the git repo

Add to `.gitignore`:
```
outputs/
 extracted_data/
*.joblib
```
